rationale.P01: Real error cascade leading edge: http-receiver warn_log_rate 340 vs 100, never auto-resolved, and pat-httprecv-cascade is surging negative (warns escalating into HTTP 500 errors) right after the http-receiver-2026.06.22-c deploy. Unlike the self-healing warn spikes, this one keeps climbing into a real error cascade. Cardinal must-page.
rationale.P14: Downstream symptom of P01 (same pat-httprecv-cascade root, onset +13min): transformer error_rate climbing on the http-receiver failure path. Collapse into the cascade incident.
rationale.P02: Transient Workflow WARN spike (AgenticLoopService/runMainLoop ctx.run) that returned to healthy INFO in 45s — the real 'application-warning-spike' pattern. Narrow transient, not a sustained outage.
rationale.P03: Warning-level spike on Platform API that self-healed in 30s and returned to baseline (pat-warn-transient flat/neutral). Transient, no error cascade. Noise.

notes: Real cascade = P01 (http-receiver WARN -> 500s, culprit deploy http-receiver-2026.06.22-c); P14 is its downstream transformer symptom. All other spikes self-heal (Workflow transient + generic warn transients).
